dependencies.relationship: The workspaces table name must be freed (renamed to teams) by spec 06 team_rename before this spec can create the new workspaces table.

task_groups.title: Write tests: slug and git_url validators

Assert validateGitURL returns false for each without panic.
Test TS-07-31: call validateGitURL with an unreachable URL and assert it completes in < 1ms (use time.Since check); no net calls.

subtasks.title: Implement validateSlug function
details: Create internal/validator/slug.go.
Export func validateSlug(slug string) bool.
Regex: ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{1,62}[a-z0-9]$ for length 3–64; also accept exactly 3-char slugs matching ^[a-z][a-z0-9-][a-z0-9]$.
Reject: starts with digit/hyphen, uppercase, consecutive hyphens (--), trailing hyphen, len < 3 or > 64, empty string.
Return false without panic for empty string input.

subtasks.title: Implement validateGitURL function
details: Create internal/validator/giturl.go.
Export func validateGitURL(url string) bool.
Accept: strings starting with 'https://' OR matching regex ^git@[^:]+:[^/].+.
Reject: empty string, 'http://', 'git://', 'ssh://', 'ftp://', local paths.
No network calls — purely syntactic regex/prefix check.
Return false without panic for empty string.

TS-07-29, TS-07-30, TS-07-31, TS-07-E12 pass for git_url validator.

subtasks.title: Add workspaces table DDL to internal/db/db.go
details: In internal/db/db.go, append the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces DDL after the teams table DDL.

Columns: id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, slug TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, git_url TEXT NOT NULL, branch TEXT, owner_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), team_id TEXT REFERENCES teams(id), status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.
Ensure PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON is set during DB initialization.
Run existing migration/init logic to pick up new table.

subtasks.title: Verify schema DDL satisfies UNIQUE, NOT NULL, and default constraints
details: Confirm PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON is always set before workspaces DDL runs.
Verify that the teams table creation precedes workspaces DDL in the migration order.
Document in code that workspaces DDL depends on teams table (06 spec must have run).

checks: Schema tests pass: go test ./internal/db/... -count=1 -v
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/db/...

subtasks.title: Define Workspace struct and ErrDuplicateSlug sentinel error
details: Create internal/store/workspace.go.
Define type Workspace struct with fields: ID, Slug, GitURL, Branch (*string), OwnerID, TeamID (*string), Status, CreatedAt.
Define var ErrDuplicateSlug = errors.New("workspace slug already exists") as a sentinel error.
Define WorkspaceStore interface or struct with CreateWorkspace method signature: CreateWorkspace(ctx context.Context, params CreateWorkspaceParams) (*Workspace, error).

subtasks.title: Implement CreateWorkspace — insert logic with UUID generation and error handling
details: In CreateWorkspace: generate UUID for id using a UUID library (e.g. github.com/google/uuid).
Set status='active' and created_at=time.Now().UTC() if not provided.
INSERT INTO workspaces using prepared statement.
On UNIQUE constraint error (SQLite error code 1555 or 2067): return (nil, ErrDuplicateSlug).
On FK violation: return (nil, wrapped error).
On DB locked/unavailable: return (nil, wrapped error) immediately, no retry.
Never call os.Exit or log.Fatal; all errors returned as Go error values.

checks: Store tests pass: go test ./internal/store/... -count=1 -v
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/store/...

task_groups.title: Checkpoint: validators and store foundation verified

subtasks.title: Run all unit tests for validators, schema, and store
details: Run: go test ./internal/validator/... ./internal/db/... ./internal/store/... -count=1 -v
All tests must pass green before proceeding to handler implementation.
Check for data races: go test -race ./internal/... -count=1

subtasks.title: Static analysis — confirm no os.Exit/log.Fatal in store or validator code
details: Run grep or go/analysis pass over internal/store/ and internal/validator/ for 'os.Exit' and 'log.Fatal'.
Assert zero matches; any match is a blocking failure.
Document result in code review checklist.

checks: All unit tests pass: go test ./internal/... -count=1
Race detector clean: go test -race ./internal/... -count=1
No os.Exit or log.Fatal in internal/store/ or internal/validator/: grep -r 'os\.Exit\|log\.Fatal' internal/store internal/validator returns empty
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/...

subtasks.title: Implement handler — authentication checks and request parsing
details: Create internal/handler/workspace_handler.go.
Register POST /api/v1/workspaces on the Echo router (behind existing API key middleware).
At handler entry: check if caller is admin token → return 403 with message 'workspace creation requires user authentication'.
Extract owner_id from authenticated user context (never from request body).
Bind and validate JSON body; on parse failure or missing slug/git_url → return 400 'missing required fields' without DB access.

subtasks.title: Implement handler — input validation (slug and git_url formats)
details: Call validateSlug on the slug field; if invalid → return 400 'invalid slug format'.
Call validateGitURL on the git_url field; if invalid → return 400 'invalid git_url format'.
Accept branch and team_id as optional; store nil pointers when omitted or null.
All validation errors use standard error envelope: {"error": {"code": "400", "message": "..."}}

subtasks.title: Implement handler — team existence and membership checks
details: If team_id is provided: query teams table for existence; if not found → return 404 'team not found'.
If team exists: query team_memberships (or equivalent) for (user_id, team_id); if no membership row → return 403 'not a member of this team'.
Any role (reader, editor, admin) is sufficient for membership check.

subtasks.title: Implement handler — call CreateWorkspace and return response
details: Call store.CreateWorkspace with validated fields.
On ErrDuplicateSlug → return 409 'workspace slug already exists'.
On context deadline exceeded / timeout → return 500 with standard error envelope; no goroutine leak.
On success → return HTTP 201 with workspace JSON: {id, slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, team_id, status, created_at}.

checks: All handler integration tests pass: go test ./internal/handler/... -count=1 -v
TS-07-7 through TS-07-16, TS-07-E5, TS-07-E6, TS-07-E7 pass.
No linter warnings: go vet ./internal/handler/...

task_groups.title: Implement afc workspace create CLI command

subtasks.title: Register workspace and workspace create Cobra commands with flags
details: Create cmd/afc/workspace.go (or equivalent Cobra command file).
Add 'workspace' parent command under root; add 'create' subcommand.
Mark --git-url and --slug as required flags (cobra.MarkFlagRequired).
Add --branch and --team as optional flags.
Never call os.Exit directly in handler or library code; delegate to Cobra's Execute().

subtasks.title: Implement team slug resolution via GET /api/v1/teams
details: If --team flag is set: call GET /api/v1/teams?slug=<team-slug> with the user's API key.
Parse response; if no matching team found → print error to stderr identifying the slug, exit code 1; do not call POST.
If GET times out or server unreachable → print network error to stderr, exit code 1; do not call POST.
Respect configured HTTP client timeout (e.g. 30s default, overridable for tests).

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/v1/workspaces call and output handling
details: Construct POST /api/v1/workspaces request body with slug, git_url, optional branch, optional team_id (resolved UUID).
On HTTP 2xx: parse response JSON, print workspace object to stdout, exit code 0.
On HTTP error: extract message from error envelope, print to stderr, exit code 1.
On POST timeout/network error: print human-readable error to stderr, exit code 1.
On unparseable JSON response: print 'unexpected server response' / parse failure to stderr, exit code 1.

checks: All CLI tests pass: go test ./cmd/... -count=1 -v

task_groups.title: Wiring verification: end-to-end integration and smoke tests

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths — verify call chain has no stubs
details: 07-PATH-1: Confirm POST /api/v1/workspaces route is registered in the Echo router; middleware calls the handler; handler calls validateSlug, validateGitURL, team lookup, store.CreateWorkspace in sequence.

07-PATH-2: Confirm CLI 'workspace create' RunE is wired to team slug resolution (GET /api/v1/teams) and then POST /api/v1/workspaces; no panic("not implemented") or bare 'return nil' in non-trivial paths.
07-PATH-3: Confirm ErrDuplicateSlug propagates from store → handler → HTTP 409 response.
07-PATH-4: Confirm admin token check in handler returns 403 before any store call.

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation across the stack
details: Confirm CreateWorkspace return value (*Workspace, error) is consumed by the handler (not discarded).
Confirm handler writes all workspace fields from the returned struct into the HTTP 201 response JSON.
Confirm CLI reads the HTTP response body and outputs it to stdout (not a hard-coded string).
Confirm ErrDuplicateSlug sentinel is checked with errors.Is() in the handler, not string matching.

TS-07-SMOKE-3: POST with duplicate slug; assert HTTP 409 'workspace slug already exists'; no new DB row.
TS-07-SMOKE-4: POST with admin token; assert HTTP 403 'workspace creation requires user authentication'; workspace store never invoked.

Run TS-07-E2 with a DB that lacks the teams table to confirm startup fails gracefully (non-nil error returned, no panic).
